description:
You are a technical writer who has just finished polishing the draft documentation for a forthcoming release.  
Your goal is to tidy things up by creating a compressed backup of the drafts, extracting that backup to a separate review location, and then recording what you did in a tiny log file.

The drafts already live in a directory that has been prepared for you:

  • /home/user/project_docs/intro.txt  
  • /home/user/project_docs/usage.txt  
  • /home/user/project_docs/changelog.txt  

Your tasks:

1. Create a sub-directory called /home/user/archives (create it if it does not exist).  
2. Inside /home/user/archives, create a gzipped tar archive that contains the entire project_docs directory.  
   • The archive must be named exactly: project_docs_20230115.tar.gz  
3. Extract the newly created archive into a new directory called /home/user/review_docs (create it if it does not exist).  
4. After completing steps 1–3, write a two-line log file named /home/user/archives/compression_log.txt with the following exact content and order (plain text, no extra spaces or blank lines):

ARCHIVE_CREATED: project_docs_20230115.tar.gz  
EXTRACTED_TO: /home/user/review_docs

Verification notes (what the automated checker will look for):
• /home/user/archives/project_docs_20230115.tar.gz must exist and be a valid gzip-compressed tar file that, when extracted, produces exactly intro.txt, usage.txt and changelog.txt inside a project_docs/ directory.  
• /home/user/review_docs/project_docs/ must exist after extraction and contain the same three files, each byte-for-byte identical to the originals.  
• /home/user/archives/compression_log.txt must exist and match the two-line format shown above with no trailing whitespace or extra newlines.

You do not need to touch the contents of the text files; only compress, extract, and log.  
A correct solution can be completed with just a couple of standard Linux commands for archiving and extracting, plus normal redirection to create the log file.
truth:
Initial directory tree and file contents available before the agent starts:

/home/user/project_docs/intro.txt  
  > Welcome to the intro.  
/home/user/project_docs/usage.txt  
  > Usage instructions go here.  
/home/user/project_docs/changelog.txt  
  > Changelog:
  > * Initial draft completed.

After the agent finishes, the following must hold true:

1. Archive file:
/home/user/archives/project_docs_20230115.tar.gz  
   – Valid gzip-compressed tar archive.  
   – Contains a single top-level directory named project_docs.  
   – Inside project_docs are intro.txt, usage.txt, changelog.txt with the exact same byte contents as listed above.

2. Extraction target:
/home/user/review_docs/project_docs/intro.txt          (identical content)  
/home/user/review_docs/project_docs/usage.txt          (identical content)  
/home/user/review_docs/project_docs/changelog.txt      (identical content)

3. Log file:
/home/user/archives/compression_log.txt  
Exact 2-line content (including newline at end of each line):

ARCHIVE_CREATED: project_docs_20230115.tar.gz\n
EXTRACTED_TO: /home/user/review_docs\n

There must be no extra blank lines, leading/trailing spaces, or additional text.
